---
title: Homarr
description: Customisable server dashboard with automatic Docker container discovery.
---

import Mermaid from '../../../components/Mermaid.astro';

**Image:** `ghcr.io/ajnart/homarr:0.15.3`  
**Port:** `7575`  
**Data:** `homarr_configs`, `homarr_icons`, `homarr_data` (named volumes)

## Introduction

[Homarr](https://homarr.dev/) is an open-source, customisable dashboard for self-hosted services. It can automatically discover running Docker containers and display service tiles with health status, links, and app integrations.

## When to Use

- You run multiple self-hosted services and want a single-pane-of-glass homepage
- You want to visually organise and access your services from a browser
- You want status indicators for your running containers without a full monitoring stack

## Architecture

<Mermaid diagram={`
graph LR
    Browser["Browser"] -->|":7575"| Homarr
    subgraph Homarr["Homarr Container"]
        UI["Web UI\n(drag & drop dashboard)"]
        Configs["homarr_configs"]
        Icons["homarr_icons"]
        Data["homarr_data"]
    end
    Homarr -->|"optional:\nDocker socket"| DockerSocket["/var/run/docker.sock\n(container discovery)"]
    DockerSocket --> Containers["Running containers\non the host"]
`} />

## Prerequisites

- Docker Engine 20.10+, Docker Compose v2
- No extra requirements. Docker socket is optional but enables auto-discovery.

## Ports

| Port | Protocol | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `7575` | HTTP | Homarr web UI |

Configurable via `HOMARR_PORT`.

## Volumes

| Volume | Contents |
|---|---|
| `homarr_configs` | Dashboard layout and service configuration |
| `homarr_icons` | Cached and uploaded service icons |
| `homarr_data` | Application state |

## Configuration

```bash
cd homarr
cp .env.example .env
```

| Variable | Default | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `HOMARR_PORT` | `7575` | Web UI port |

No other variables are required for basic use.

## Workflow

```bash
# Start
docker compose up -d

# Open UI
open http://localhost:7575

# Add services via Settings → Services in the UI
# Drag tiles to rearrange them

# Stop
docker compose down

# Stop and remove all data (layout, icons, state)
docker compose down -v
```

## Use Cases

**Service homepage:** Add tiles for every service in this repo — Portainer, Seq, Jenkins, SonarQube, Kafka UI — and access them all from one URL.

**Container status board:** With the Docker socket mounted, Homarr shows whether each container is running, stopped, or in error — without opening Portainer.

**Team portal:** Share the Homarr URL with your team as the entry point to all internal tools.

## Security

:::caution[Docker socket]
The template mounts `/var/run/docker.sock` for container discovery. This gives the Homarr container root-equivalent access to the Docker host. If you don't need auto-discovery, remove the socket mount from `docker-compose.yml`.
:::

## Integration

### Nginx Proxy Manager

Uncomment the NPM block in `docker-compose.yml`, restart, and create a proxy host: `dashboard.yourdomain.com → homarr:7575`.

### Portainer

Deploy as a Portainer stack. Set `HOMARR_PORT` in the Environment variables UI if the default conflicts.

## References

- [Homarr documentation](https://homarr.dev/docs/getting-started/installation)
